### To do!

- [x] [original paper by Henon and Heiles](https://articles.adsabs.harvard.edu/pdf/1964AJ.....69...73H) @Henon1964


**yesterdays todo's:***

- [ ] investigation of 'How to implement?' and 'How to visiualize?'
- [ ] simple implementation of various and visualization 

---

### checking in

I feel that I should read about implementing the Hénon & Heiles. 
I should also read their original paper.

Whilst working on Hénon & Heiles, I realize I need to get an in depth understanding of the Lagrange and Hamiltonian formalism!

### Notes from Hénon & Heiles
@Henon1964
- on isolating and non isolating integrals [A CLASSIFICATION OF THE INTEGRALS OF MOTION by
George Contopoulos](https://adsabs.harvard.edu/pdf/1963ApJ...138.1297C)


::: {layout-ncol=1}
![Citation @Henon1964: On the argument of 2 or 3 non isolating integrals.](/pic/2026-05-28-01-henon_heiles.png)
:::

- I did not understand much of section 2
- a second read and note shed some light, here is an interesting paragraph describing what ergodic mean

::: {layout-ncol=1}
![Citation @Henon1964: On finding the second Integral. Leading to an understanding of what it means to be ergodic.](/pic/2026-05-28-03-HenonHeiles-ergodic.png)
:::

- a question they seem to be answering is how to find a proof of the existence of an non isolating integral

- they speak of two integration schemes: 
  - Adams
  - Runge-Kutta

- Their analysis introduces terms as *islands* and *chain of islands*, *dimension of islands*, *dense* and need to be understood when I study this model

::: {layout-ncol=1}
![Citation @Henon1964: Statements and potential open questions.](/pic/2026-05-28-05-HenonHeiles-openquestion.png)
:::

- "but the dimensions are so small that finding them is difficult" – is how the term dimensions gets used again
